chunk-001.decision_context: The team is under enterprise latency pressure while debating Helix modular-monolith versus splitting core services now.
chunk-001.implied_decision: Whether to pursue Helix modular-monolith or split core services now.
goals: Reduce checkout and invoice latency for enterprise accounts
Present a phased architecture and cost story to finance and exec staff
options_considered: Extract payments and catalog services into microservices now
Adopt Helix modular monolith and defer service split
constraints: Finance wants phased infrastructure spend, not a big-bang budget hit
risks: Incorrect bottleneck diagnosis leading to the wrong architecture move

evidence.text: P95 checkout latency remains above the 800ms target on peak days
Enterprise accounts raised latency complaints again this quarter

goals: Enable safer independent deploys without overwhelming the platform team
options_considered: Stay on the monolith and invest only in performance guardrails
Hybrid Helix core plus one extracted invoice worker service
constraints: 14-person platform team with flat SRE headcount
Sales messaging must not promise a rewrite timeline the team cannot keep
risks: Operational overload if microservices are introduced too early
assumptions: Lighthouse slowdowns may reflect invoice generation rather than checkout alone

evidence.text: Two lighthouse customers reported slowdowns during invoice generation rather than browsing
Microservices on day one would double operational load for a 14-person platform team

open_questions: What is the true bottleneck for invoice latency?
Can Helix deliver independent deploys without new clusters?
tensions: Independent deploy goals vs flat SRE headcount and on-call load
missing_context: Verified root cause for lighthouse slowdowns

conflicts.statementB: Priya wants Helix modular monolith first

open_questions: Do we have SRE capacity for microservices operations?
What budget envelope will finance approve?
tensions: Near-term invoice remediation vs long-term re-architecture messaging
suggested_next_steps: Define Helix contract boundaries and ownership

unresolved_references.note: Internal platform codename, target modular-monolith shape, and migration program name are used interchangeably and remain unresolved

goals: Meet a credible September performance remediation milestone
risks: Vague Helix meaning slowing alignment and customer comms
Repeating last year's big-refactor morale hit
assumptions: Module boundaries will be ignored without tooling enforcement
chunk-004.recommendation_candidate: Pursue Helix modular-monolith with a staged invoice remediation track, paired with tracing and SLO gates, while keeping microservices extraction as a later phase if module boundaries fail.
suggested_next_steps: Publish a one-page decision framing for exec staff
Model phased infrastructure cost for finance review
Add tracing coverage requirements before migration kickoff

evidence.text: Checkout P95 was 940ms on Tuesday peak in last week's dashboard
Invoice generation queue depth doubled during the backfill window on Thursday
Northwind asked whether modular-monolith means the same uptime promises and the team did not answer

open_questions: Does modular-monolith preserve the same uptime promises customers expect?
missing_context: Northwind uptime promise expectations for modular-monolith
suggested_next_steps: Draft customer messaging focused on remediation milestones rather than architecture jargon
assumptions: Invoice batch guardrails can buy time if the backfill issue is fixed
